content.Text: [Wed 2026-04-22 12:09 PDT] Create `docs/partitioning.html` - an Interactive Graph Partitioning Visualizer for the GraphVisual project. ## What to build An interactive tool that partitions a graph into 2 (or k) groups minimizing edges between groups (min-cut). Features: - **Kernighan-Lin algorithm** with step-by-step animation showing vertex swaps - **Random partitioning** as baseline comparison - **Spectral partitioning** using Fiedler vector (algebraic connectivity) - Canvas visualization with nodes colored by partition, edges colored by cut/internal - Drag nodes to manually reassign partitions - Stats: cut size, balance ratio, modularity - 8 presets (Karate Club, Barbell, Grid, Complete Bipartite, Random, Caveman, Butterfly, Custom text input) - Animation speed control, step-by-step or auto-play - Autonomous feature: **Auto-Partition Advisor** that runs all 3 algorithms, compares results, and recommends the best partition with explanation - JSON export of partition assignments ## Style/Pattern Follow the EXACT same HTML structure, CSS variables, sidebar nav, and layout patterns as `docs/independent-set.html`. Use the same CSS classes (btn, btn-primary, btn-secondary, control-group, stat-card, preset-btn, etc.). Link to `styles.css`. Use Canvas for graph rendering. Dark theme with CSS variables (--surface, --border, --accent, --text, --bg, etc.). Copy the full sidebar navigation from independent-set.html and add a new entry for partitioning: `✂️Partitioning` Place it after the Self-Repair link in the sidebar Tools section. ## Important - Single self-contained HTML file (all JS inline) - No external dependencies beyond styles.css - Must work standalone in browser - Canvas with drag support for nodes - Step animation with play/pause/step controls Also update `docs/index.html` to add a link/card for the new Partitioning tool alongside the other tools. Look at how existing tools are listed and add a matching entry with icon ✂️ and description "Interactive graph partitioning with Kernighan-Lin, spectral, and random algorithms. Auto-advisor recommends optimal partition." Do NOT create any new branches or PRs. Just make the changes to the files.
I’m inspecting the existing docs layout first so the new page matches the project’s established HTML structure, sidebar, and component classes exactly.
I have the overall layout. Next I’m pulling the exact sidebar block and the index card pattern so the new page slots into the existing docs without introducing any structural drift.

`independent-set.html` appears to have a shorter tool list than the current index, so I’m checking the rest of the docs for the actual `Self-Repair` entry the user referenced and for the canonical card pattern used by recent tools.

ToolUse.name: Run Get-ChildItem -Path docs -Filter *.html | Select-String -Pattern 'Self-Repair|self-repair|Partitioning|Independent Set Finder|card-grid' | ForEach-Object { "{0}:{1}:{2}" -f $_.Path, $_.LineNumber, $_.Line.Trim() }

🏝️ Maximum Independent Set Finder Find largest independent vertex sets with greedy, exact & complement-clique algorithms

An independent set S of a graph G is a set of vertices where no two are adjacent. The Maximum Independent Set (MIS) is the largest such set — its size is denoted α(G). Finding it is NP-hard, but this tool lets you visualize three different approaches step by step.

In independent set

Available

Presets

Algorithm

Custom Input

Step History

Theorems

α(G) + β(G) = n — Gallai's theorem (α = independence #, β = vertex cover #)
α(G) = ω(Ḡ) — independence # = clique # of complement
Run an algorithm to see computed values.

Export

📊 GraphVisual Community evolution visualization for student social networks

🚀 Get Started 📖 API Reference 🍳Cookbook ⭐ GitHub

What is GraphVisual?

GraphVisual is a Java desktop application for studying community evolution in student populations using Bluetooth proximity data. It processes meeting records from a PostgreSQL database, classifies social relationships, and renders interactive graph visualizations with timeline playback.

Built for research on social network analysis — specifically understanding how communities form, dissolve, and evolve over time in university settings.

Features

🎨

Interactive Visualization

Drag, zoom, and rotate graph nodes using JUNG's graph mouse. Explore community structures with cluster-based layouts.

⏱️

Timeline Playback

Animate community graphs across 92 days with play/pause/stop controls. Watch relationships form and dissolve.

🏷️

5 Relationship Types

Friends, classmates, study groups, familiar strangers, and strangers — each color-coded on the graph.

🎛️

Adjustable Thresholds

Tune meeting duration and frequency thresholds per relationship type in real-time.

📊

Network Statistics

Real-time metrics: node/edge counts, density, degree stats, hub identification, centrality analysis.

🔬

Community Detection

Connected-component analysis with modularity scoring, community density, and dominant edge-type breakdowns.

🧬

Community Evolution Tracker

Watch communities form, merge, split, and dissolve over time with autonomous event detection and proactive fragility warnings.

🧭

Shortest Path Finder

BFS (hop-count) and Dijkstra (weight-optimal) path finding between any two nodes.

💾

GraphML Export

Export graphs to standard GraphML format for Gephi, Cytoscape, NetworkX, and other tools.

🦠

Diffusion Simulator

Simulate epidemic and information spreading on networks with SIR, SIS, and Independent Cascade models. Proactive containment recommendations.

🏝️

Independent Set Finder

Find maximum independent sets with greedy, exact backtracking, and complement-clique algorithms. Step-by-step animation with Gallai theorem verification.

Relationship Classification

TypeColorLocationDurationFrequency
FriendsGreenPublic areas> 10 min≥ 2/day
ClassmatesBlueClassrooms> 30 min≥ 1/day
Study GroupsOrangeClassrooms> 20 min≤ 1/day
Familiar StrangersGrayPublic/paths< 2 min> 1/day
StrangersRedPublic/paths< 2 min< 2/day

All thresholds are adjustable at runtime via the Category Panel sliders.

Quick Start

# Clone
git clone https://github.com/sauravbhattacharya001/GraphVisual.git
cd GraphVisual

# Configure database
export DB_HOST=localhost DB_USER=your_user DB_PASS=your_pass

# Build with Maven
mvn initialize -P install-local-deps
mvn package -B

# Run
java -jar target/graphvisual-1.1.0-all.jar
See the full setup guide for detailed instructions including Docker, Ant builds, and data pipeline setup.

Tech Stack

TechnologyPurpose
Java 11+Application language
JUNG 2.0.1Graph data structures and visualization
Java SwingDesktop GUI framework
PostgreSQLMeeting and Bluetooth event storage
MavenBuild and dependency management
JUnit 4Unit testing
GitHub ActionsCI/CD, CodeQL, Pages deployment

Documentation

🚀

Setup Guide

Installation, configuration, building, running the data pipeline, Docker deployment.

📊 GraphVisual Community evolution visualization for student social networks

🚀 Get Started 📖 API Reference 🍳Cookbook ⭐ GitHub

What is GraphVisual?

GraphVisual is a Java desktop application for studying community evolution in student populations using Bluetooth proximity data. It processes meeting records from a PostgreSQL database, classifies social relationships, and renders interactive graph visualizations with timeline playback.

Built for research on social network analysis — specifically understanding how communities form, dissolve, and evolve over time in university settings.

Features

🎨

Interactive Visualization

Drag, zoom, and rotate graph nodes using JUNG's graph mouse. Explore community structures with cluster-based layouts.

⏱️

Timeline Playback

Animate community graphs across 92 days with play/pause/stop controls. Watch relationships form and dissolve.

🏷️

5 Relationship Types

Friends, classmates, study groups, familiar strangers, and strangers — each color-coded on the graph.

🎛️

Adjustable Thresholds

Tune meeting duration and frequency thresholds per relationship type in real-time.

📊

Network Statistics

Real-time metrics: node/edge counts, density, degree stats, hub identification, centrality analysis.

🔬

Community Detection

Connected-component analysis with modularity scoring, community density, and dominant edge-type breakdowns.

🧬

Community Evolution Tracker

Watch communities form, merge, split, and dissolve over time with autonomous event detection and proactive fragility warnings.

🧭

Shortest Path Finder

BFS (hop-count) and Dijkstra (weight-optimal) path finding between any two nodes.

💾

GraphML Export

Export graphs to standard GraphML format for Gephi, Cytoscape, NetworkX, and other tools.

🦠

Diffusion Simulator

Simulate epidemic and information spreading on networks with SIR, SIS, and Independent Cascade models. Proactive containment recommendations.

🏝️

Independent Set Finder

Find maximum independent sets with greedy, exact backtracking, and complement-clique algorithms. Step-by-step animation with Gallai theorem verification.

Relationship Classification

TypeColorLocationDurationFrequency
FriendsGreenPublic areas> 10 min≥ 2/day
ClassmatesBlueClassrooms> 30 min≥ 1/day
Study GroupsOrangeClassrooms> 20 min≤ 1/day
Familiar StrangersGrayPublic/paths< 2 min> 1/day
StrangersRedPublic/paths< 2 min< 2/day

All thresholds are adjustable at runtime via the Category Panel sliders.

Quick Start

# Clone
git clone https://github.com/sauravbhattacharya001/GraphVisual.git
cd GraphVisual

# Configure database
export DB_HOST=localhost DB_USER=your_user DB_PASS=your_pass

# Build with Maven
mvn initialize -P install-local-deps
mvn package -B

# Run
java -jar target/graphvisual-1.1.0-all.jar
See the full setup guide for detailed instructions including Docker, Ant builds, and data pipeline setup.

Tech Stack

TechnologyPurpose
Java 11+Application language
JUNG 2.0.1Graph data structures and visualization
Java SwingDesktop GUI framework
PostgreSQLMeeting and Bluetooth event storage
MavenBuild and dependency management
JUnit 4Unit testing
GitHub ActionsCI/CD, CodeQL, Pages deployment

Documentation

🚀

Setup Guide

Installation, configuration, building, running the data pipeline, Docker deployment.
